summary: Comprehensive testing of RBAC and permission enforcement features for Miles Capitals FX Broker. All 17 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI verified working with all Settings, Users, and Roles & Permissions features accessible.

issues: Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' when opening role dialogs - accessibility improvement needed

verified: Admin can access /settings page
User Management tab displays 7 users with roles
Email Reports tab accessible with SMTP settings, director emails, daily report scheduler
Commission & FX tab accessible with FX rates
GET /api/roles/exchanger returns role with permissions
PUT /api/roles/exchanger successfully updates permissions
Permission changes immediately affect vendor user access
Removing 'approve' permission returns 403 on vendor endpoints
Restoring 'approve' permission returns 404 (access granted, transaction not found)
apscheduler is running in backend (confirmed in logs)
send_daily_report job scheduled at 00:00 UTC
Email settings API accessible with report_enabled, report_time, director_emails
POST /api/reports/send-now endpoint exists for manual sending

migration_endpoint.verified: Assigns role_ids to existing users based on role field

verified: Roles page shows 6 system roles: Super Admin, Admin, Accountant, Sub Admin, Exchanger, Viewer
Each role displays permission count, module access badges, hierarchy level
Edit button opens permission matrix dialog
New Role button opens creation form with full permission matrix
Permission matrix has columns: View, Create, Edit, Approve, Export
Users table displays 7 users with roles (ADMIN, ACCOUNTANT, EXCHANGER)
Add User button present
Role badges displayed with correct colors
Status column shows Active/Inactive

critical_code_review_comments: Good: vendor_approve_transaction now uses require_permission(Modules.TRANSACTIONS, Actions.APPROVE) instead of require_vendor
Good: Permission check happens before business logic validation in vendor endpoints
Good: role_id field properly falls back to role field for backward compatibility
Minor: Dialog components should add aria-describedby for better accessibility
context_for_next_testing_agent: RBAC permission enforcement is working. Vendor endpoints (approve_transaction, reject_transaction, approve_ie, reject_ie) now use require_permission decorator. The assign-role-ids migration endpoint can be called to assign role_ids to users who only have the legacy 'role' field. Daily P&L email scheduler runs via apscheduler at 00:00 UTC.

rca_of_the_issue.P0_settings_page: Issue was not reproducing - Settings page loads correctly for admin users
rca_of_the_issue.P1_exchanger_permissions: Fixed by updating vendor endpoints to use require_permission decorator instead of require_vendor. Permission changes now immediately enforced via get_user_permissions() which checks role_id then role field
rca_of_the_issue.P2_daily_pnl_email: Already implemented via apscheduler - send_daily_report job includes P&L data in email
